name: Connect to Remote Server with Auth
query: Connect me to my Conductor server at https://conductor.mycompany.com/api
expected_behavior: Step 1: Check if `conductor` CLI is installed, install if missing
Step 2: Set CONDUCTOR_SERVER_URL to the provided URL
Step 3: Test connectivity with `conductor workflow list`
Step 4: If 401/403 error, inform user the server requires authentication
Step 5: Ask user for auth key and secret (or token)
Step 6: Set CONDUCTOR_AUTH_KEY and CONDUCTOR_AUTH_SECRET (or CONDUCTOR_AUTH_TOKEN)
Step 7: Retry `conductor workflow list` to verify auth works
Step 8: Offer to save as a profile: `conductor config save --server <url> --auth-key <key> --auth-secret <secret> --profile <name>`
Step 9: Confirm connection is working and report workflow count
success_criteria: Server URL is set exactly as provided by user
Auth is not assumed — connectivity is tested first
Auth credentials are only requested after receiving 401/403
Credentials are set via env vars or --auth-key/--auth-secret flags
Connection is verified with a real API call after auth setup
User is offered to save the config as a profile for future use
Plan does not include commands that would print auth keys, secrets, or tokens to stdout
